symptom: Laser is not cutting all the way through the material
causes: Power too low
Speed too high
Insufficient passes
Incorrect focus height
Material is thicker or denser than the current settings assume
fixes: Run a small test cut on scrap before committing to the full job
Re-check focus distance for the actual material thickness
Increase power and/or reduce speed
Add an additional pass

symptom: Engraving comes out too light / faint
causes: Power too low for the material
Speed too high
Focus slightly off
On bitmap engraves, Dot Duration set too short on fiber/IR jobs
fixes: Run a Material Test Array to dial in optimal values rather than guessing
Re-focus
Increase power percentage and decrease speed
For bitmap/fiber jobs, increase Dot Duration (recommended above 100µs)

symptom: Engraving is too dark, scorched, or shows excessive charring
causes: Power too high or speed too low for the material
Too many passes
Material carbonizes/burns at the current energy density (especially MDF and some plywoods)
fixes: Reduce power and/or increase speed
Reduce pass count
For MDF specifically, favor a higher-speed, multi-pass approach over a single very-slow, high-power pass to reduce charring

symptom: Focus issues — engraving/cutting is blurry, inconsistent, or the cut fails only in parts of the design
causes: Manual focus tool not used correctly
Material surface is not flat/level
Auto-focus (on supported machines) failed to register true material height (e.g., on reflective, transparent, or unusually textured material)
fixes: Re-level the material and re-run focus
For manual-focus machines, use the physical focus tool/gauge exactly per the material's actual thickness at the point being processed
For auto-focus machines, retry the auto-focus routine or fall back to manual focus if auto-focus repeatedly fails on a difficult material

symptom: Machine will not connect over USB
causes: Faulty/incompatible USB cable
Driver not installed
Wrong USB port
The software wasn't fully closed/reopened after a previous failed connection attempt
fixes: Use the cable that shipped with the machine (or a verified data-capable replacement, not a charge-only cable)
Try a different USB port (avoid unpowered hubs)
Reinstall/update USB drivers if prompted
Fully quit and relaunch XCS/Studio
Power-cycle the machine

symptom: Machine will not connect over Wi-Fi
causes: Machine and controlling device are on different Wi-Fi networks/bands (many xTool machines only support 2.4GHz, not 5GHz)
Incorrect Wi-Fi password entered during setup
Router firewall/AP isolation blocking local device discovery
fixes: Confirm the router network being joined is 2.4GHz (create a dedicated 2.4GHz SSID if the router only broadcasts a combined/5GHz-preferred network)
Re-run the in-app Wi-Fi setup wizard and re-enter the password carefully
Temporarily disable AP/client isolation on the router if enabled
Ensure the controlling device is on the same network/subnet as the machine

symptom: Fire, flare-ups, or flame during processing
causes: Excess power/too-slow speed causing sustained combustion rather than clean ablation
Processing a prohibited/flammable material
Processing material thicker than the machine's safe rated maximum (e.g., P2 plywood over 12mm)
Leaving the machine unattended while it smolders after a job
fixes: Immediately stop the job
If flame is visible, use an appropriate fire extinguisher (do not use water on an electrical/laser fire) or smother small flare-ups
Never leave a running laser unattended
Never exceed official maximum-thickness guidance for a given material/machine
Reduce power/increase speed for future runs of the same material
Ensure ventilation/air assist is functioning to blow combustion byproducts away from the cut kerf

symptom: Misalignment between the design preview/frame and the actual cut/engrave position
causes: Camera calibration drift (on camera-equipped machines like S1)
Material moved after framing but before processing
Material is not perfectly flat/seated in the work area
fixes: Avoid touching/shifting material between framing and processing
Re-frame immediately before processing rather than relying on an earlier frame
Use a honeycomb panel, riser base, or magnets/pins to secure material flat and stationary
Re-run camera calibration per the machine's calibration routine if using camera-assisted positioning

symptom: Material scorching / burn marks around the edges of a cut or engrave
causes: Excess power
Insufficient air assist
Residual heat buildup from slow speed/multiple passes without adequate cooling between passes
fixes: Verify air assist is enabled and the nozzle/airflow path is clear
Reduce power and/or increase speed
On multi-pass jobs, allow brief cooling if scorching is severe
For wood specifically, masking tape (blue painter's tape) over the surface can reduce visible edge scorching on engraves

symptom: Software freezes, crashes, or becomes unresponsive during a job
causes: Outdated software version
Corrupted project file
Insufficient system resources (very large bitmap images)
A USB/Wi-Fi communication drop mid-job that the UI doesn't recover from gracefully
fixes: Save work frequently as separate project files to avoid losing progress to a crash
Update to the latest XCS or Studio version
Reduce imported image resolution/file size where possible
Restart the software and machine before retrying

symptom: Machine detects an error code and halts (e.g., lid open, protection triggered)
causes: Safety interlock triggered (lid/cover opened mid-job on enclosed machines like S1)
A specific protection error code such as S1's fire-risk M53 F5 (small design area + high power + low speed)
fixes: Close the lid/cover fully and resume/restart the job
If a specific error code appears, look it up in the machine's error-code reference article and follow the documented remedy
For M53 F5 specifically: reduce power, increase speed, or enlarge the design's bounding area to fall outside the risk threshold

symptom: Colors/vector layers imported from a design file are misassigned to the wrong processing type
causes: The design file's original color-to-layer mapping doesn't match the software's expected color convention (each distinct stroke/fill color typically maps to a separate processing layer)
fixes: Before import, standardize distinct colors in the source vector file for each intended processing type (e.g., red stroke = cut, blue stroke = score, black fill = engrave)
After import, manually reassign any mismapped layer's processing type in the software's layer panel

symptom: Bitmap/photo engrave looks banded, streaky, or has visible line artifacts
causes: DPI too low for the level of detail attempted
Wrong bitmap mode selected for the laser type in use (e.g., using plain grayscale mode on a fiber/IR job instead of Jarvis)
Bi-directional scanning introducing slight registration offset on a particular machine/material combination
fixes: Use the bitmap mode recommended for the laser type (Jarvis for infrared/fiber, grayscale for blue diode)
Increase DPI for finer detail
Switch to uni-directional scanning if banding persists

symptom: Cutting/engraving works fine on one material but fails or looks wrong on a visually similar one
causes: Materials that look alike (e.g., cast vs. extruded acrylic, or different plywood species/adhesive types) can have very different laser absorption properties
Settings tuned for one will not transfer directly to the other
fixes: Always run a Material Test Array or small scrap test when switching to a new/different material batch, even if it looks similar to a previously-used one
Consult the Material Settings Library for the specific material sub-type rather than assuming a generic category setting will work

symptom: Machine won't power on, or powers on but the laser module doesn't fire at all
causes: Power supply/adapter fault
Safety interlock not satisfied (lid open, key-lock switch off on machines that have one)
The laser module itself is disconnected/faulty
fixes: Confirm the power adapter is fully seated and the outlet is live
Ensure all lid/cover interlocks are closed and any physical key-switch is in the "on"/unlocked position
Check the laser module's cable connection for damage or looseness
If the module still doesn't fire after these checks, contact xTool support for a possible hardware fault
